outcome_text: Potential costs, including non-monetary costs, which result from expected or realized AI errors or system functionality and trustworthiness—as connected to organizational risk tolerance—are examined and documented.
scoring_text: Skill in AI cost-risk analysis, including evaluating monetary and non-monetary costs of AI errors, assessing trustworthiness failures against organizational risk tolerance, and documenting the relationship between system functionality, error rates, and organizational impact.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI cost-risk analysis, including evaluating monetary and non-monetary costs of AI errors, assessing trustworthiness failures against organizational risk tolerance, and documenting the relationship between system functionality, error rates, and organizational impact.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in communicating AI risk findings to technical and non-technical audiences, translating statistical risk measures into business impact terms.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-3.2 (MAP) superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-001 is the strongest match (STS: 0.553, cosine: 0.680). Secondary: AG-S-005 (AG, cosine: 0.674), RM-A-001 (RM, cosine: 0.669).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.5/10 (raw 0.552). Shared vocabulary: [ai, impact, non, risk, skill]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [against, analysis, assessing, cost, costs, documenting] vs WIDAI terms [audiences, business, communicating, findings, measures, statistical] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.888 (P=0.882, R=0.895). Balanced P/R — the 33-word competency implication and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.3%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.680 > STS 0.552 (gap +0.127). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, impact, non, risk, skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, impact, non, risk, skill]. NIST AI RMF-only: [against, analysis, assessing, cost, costs, documenting]. WIDAI-only: [audiences, business, communicating, findings, measures, statistical].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.127 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
